visual_features.overall_style: Corporate, high‑contrast layout with a strong black/red brand presence. Visuals lean more classic than ultra-modern: flat colors, sharp rectangular shapes, and simple iconography. The design conveys reliability and seriousness appropriate for a construction/engineering firm, with a slightly dated but solid aesthetic.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is established primarily through color contrast (black header + bright red accents), size, and positioning. 1) Brand/logo area and contact info on the black header are the strongest focal points due to high contrast and large typographic scale. 2) The horizontal navigation bar forms the second tier, with the active 'NEWS' item highlighted in red. 3) The main content image (project/people photo) is a large central focal block drawing attention after the header and nav. 4) Secondary items like top utility buttons (Contact Us, Careers, social icons) are smaller but still noticeable due to red backgrounds on a light grey strip at the very top.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Top bars (utility and header) are fairly dense with limited vertical whitespace, while the main content area below the navigation uses generous side margins and spacing around the central image, providing a clear content stage. Sections are separated by full-width color bands (light grey, black, dark grey, white) rather than by large vertical gaps, so whitespace is moderate: not cramped, but not ultra-spacious.
unique_elements: Full-width, stacked top utility bar (light grey with red CTA blocks) above a solid black brand header
Large logo area with red feather icon and wide-letterspaced wordmark 'ANTARES'
Contact information (phone + address) styled as prominent hero elements within the black header, using icons and dual-line text
Navigation bar directly under header with flat dark grey background and red active state for the current page
Large central project/team photo framed by substantial side whitespace, acting as the main content hero

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12‑column style grid with full-width header bands and a centered content column inside max-width constraints.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Stacked full-width bands (utility bar → main header → navigation → content) with centered content within each band. Main content follows a single-column, center-aligned pattern.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: From top to bottom: (1) Light grey utility strip with left-aligned CTAs (Contact Us, Careers) and right-aligned social/email icons. (2) Black brand header containing logo on the left and contact info (phone, address) right-aligned. (3) Dark grey navigation bar with horizontal menu items centered or evenly spaced. (4) White main content area with a large central image occupying the main horizontal space. Future sections would likely continue vertically under this image in the same central column.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Strong left-right alignment in the header (logo left, contact info right) within a centered overall wrapper. Navigation items are horizontally aligned and evenly spaced in a single row. Main image is center-aligned in the viewport with equal side margins, creating a symmetrical composition.
responsive_hints: Stacked band structure (top utility → header → nav → content) is naturally mobile-friendly and would likely collapse into vertical blocks.
Header content (logo vs contact info) suggests a two-column layout on desktop that would stack into single column on smaller screens.
Horizontal navigation is likely to collapse into a hamburger/menu button on mobile; the simple flat design makes conversion to a slide-out or dropdown menu straightforward.
Central content column with generous margins suggests a max-width container that would become full-width on small devices.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top‑to‑bottom flow with full-width color bands at the top (utility bar, header, navigation), followed by a centered single content column. Each upper band is visually distinct by background color: light grey utility bar, black header, dark grey nav, then white content area. The main visible content block is a large landscape image centered in the white area, likely followed by text or additional media below in a similar centered fashion.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Top ~20–25% of the viewport is occupied by the stacked header structure (light grey utility bar, black brand bar, dark grey navigation). The remaining ~75–80% is the main content area, with the hero image taking most of the central vertical space and wide side margins providing breathing room. In the header, the logo occupies roughly the left 30–40% while the contact information spans the remaining right portion. Utility buttons and social icons at the very top are compact, using a smaller vertical height than the main header.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Header and navigation are moderately dense, maximizing important information (contact info, address, nav links) in a relatively tight vertical space. The main content below is more spacious, with a large central image and significant white margins. Overall, the layout feels structured and slightly compact at the top, opening into a more airy presentation for the main content.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye follows an F/Z‑pattern: starting at the top-left with the logo and utility CTAs, moving horizontally across the black header to the prominent phone and address on the right, then down to the navigation bar scanning left-to-right across menu items. From the active red 'NEWS' menu item, the eye naturally drops to the large central image. The strong horizontal bands guide downward movement, while color contrast (red highlights and black header) anchors attention at key interaction points.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons in the top utility bar are flat, solid red rectangles with sharp 90° corners. They have white icons (envelope, checkmark) and white label text, with slight internal horizontal padding (approx. 12–16px) and a compact vertical height (~32–36px). They sit directly next to each other with no visible rounded corners or shadows, creating a strong, slightly blocky CTA row.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Most likely implemented as anchor tags styled as buttons (`Contact Us`), possibly inside a flex container for left-right alignment. Icon + text composition suggests either inline `` icon elements or background-image icons preceding text within the same link.

ui_patterns.visual_description: While explicit cards are not shown, the main content image behaves like a simple content block: centered, framed by whitespace, and optionally bounded by a thin border or dark edge on some sides. The general pattern inferred is flat containers with subtle borders rather than heavy shadows, maintaining the overall flat aesthetic.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Typical card structure would be a `div.card` with optional image at top (``), followed by title and text (`

`, `

`). Borders or background colors would differentiate card boundaries instead of raised shadows.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No form is directly visible, but based on the visual language, inputs would likely be full-width fields with rectangular sharp corners, light borders (medium grey), and minimal or no shadows. Labels likely appear above inputs, left-aligned. Buttons would match the red CTA style, using solid red with white text.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms would use stacked label-input pairs (`<label>` above `<input>` or `<textarea>`) inside vertical form groups (`<div class="form-group">`), spanning the content column width. Validation feedback would probably appear as red text under fields, leveraging the existing red brand color.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal bar with a dark grey background. Menu items are uppercase white text, evenly spaced, likely with increased letter-spacing for clarity. The active item ('NEWS') is styled in red text while others are white, with no visible underline; the background remains flat and uniform. Icons are used minimally (a small home icon before 'HOME'). The presence of a black bar above reinforces separation from the logo/header.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Most likely a semantic `
` containing an unordered list: `
  • Home
    • ...
`. Items are displayed via flexbox (`display:flex; justify-content:center;`) or inline-block for horizontal alignment. Active state is handled via a class like `.active` changing text color to red.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The visible list-like pattern is the horizontal menu: low density, ample horizontal spacing, no per-item separators except spacing and text color. Additional lists (e.g., projects, news) would likely be clean vertical stacks with clear headings and modest spacing, separated by whitespace rather than heavy rules.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Lists are expected as semantic `<ul>` or `<ol>` elements. For media-style lists (e.g., news), a flexbox 'media object' pattern might be used – small thumbnail on left, text on right – though not visible here. The navigation list uses `<li>` elements styled horizontally.

heading.visual_style: The 'ANTARES' wordmark and nav headings appear as a clean, slightly condensed geometric or neo-grotesque sans-serif with high legibility and some letterspacing, giving a precise, engineered feel.

body.visual_style: Body text (seen in smaller labels like 'Call Us', address lines) appears to be a neutral, straightforward sans-serif with good readability at smaller sizes.

spacing_system.component_padding: Navigation items and buttons use approximately 8–12px vertical and 16–24px horizontal padding. Header content (logo, contact info blocks) likely use 16–24px padding within the container.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major bands (utility bar, header, nav, content) is minimal because separation is primarily via background color. Within the white content area, sections and large blocks (like images) are separated by ~32–48px of vertical margin to maintain clarity.

interaction_hints: Buttons and nav items likely have hover states where background or text color shifts: red buttons may darken slightly or add subtle underlines; nav links may change from white to red or show an underline to indicate hover.
Active navigation items already use red text; hover could use the same or a lighter/darker red variation for consistency.
Top utility buttons may show a slight darkening of the red background or change opacity on hover, without adding shadows to maintain flat design.
Transitions, if present, would be subtle (150–250ms color transitions) rather than large animated movements, aligning with the conservative corporate look.
Micro‑interactions around contact info (phone number, address) could include click-to-call and click-to-open-map with simple hover cues such as cursor change and underline.
